mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate relaxes financing conditions and lifts equity discount valuations, so S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days are positive when policy is moving toward easier stance.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in a majority of months when FEDFUNDS is falling over the prior 3 months, or if rolling Rank IC stays below 0.03 for any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE identifies accelerating labor market stress and demand destruction; when unemployment jumps, earnings expectations weaken and S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days turn negative.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes produce positive 20-day SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the mean forward return is not below the unconditional average by at least 1 standard error.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts followed by weaker retail sales signals broad household demand retrenchment; that combination should precede negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as revenue growth slows.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint signal does not outperform either proxy alone in walk-forward tests, or if 60-day forward returns are not negative after simultaneous declines in HOUST and RSAFS.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in INDPRO indicates output contraction and inventory liquidation, which normally pressures cyclicals and the index; the effect should be negative for S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if falling INDPRO is not associated with below-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the sign of the return response flips positive in a persistent rolling window longer than 1 year.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 growth is positive while PAYEMS is not deteriorating, the economy is still expanding and equity risk premia compress; S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days should be positive.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined growth signal does not produce positive 60-day forward returns above the unconditional baseline, or if the interaction term adds no explanatory value versus GDPC1 alone.

mechanism_narrative: A low and stable CPIAUCSL combined with a flat or falling FEDFUNDS rate signals disinflation with easing policy, which supports equity multiples and should yield positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls below its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not show positive 5-day returns relative to baseline, or if inflation-plus-easing episodes fail to beat a CPI-only or FEDFUNDS-only specification.

mechanism_narrative: In a recessionary labor regime, a rising UNRATE confirms layoffs and weaker household income, so the market should sell off; S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days are negative once unemployment is already elevated.
failure_condition: Reject if elevated-unemployment episodes do not deliver negative 20-day returns, or if the conditional hit rate is not materially worse than the unconditional distribution.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in RSAFS together with weak HOUST indicates household spending and construction demand are both contracting; that combined demand shock should depress S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both fall for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Reject if the paired decline does not precede negative 60-day returns more often than either series alone, or if the joint signal is not statistically distinguishable from zero.
